title: Delicate Dutch Sirloin Steak Tips Pasta
description: A low-sodium Dutch dish featuring sirloin steak tips and pasta in a light emulsion sauce, perfect for a brunch mood. This recipe is designed to be jammy and glassy-jelly in texture.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat and cook the sirloin steak tips until browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Remove the steak tips from the skillet and set aside, then add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, dried thyme, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Return the steak tips to the skillet and simmer the mixture over medium-low heat for 5-7 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the steak is cooked to desired doneness.
Drain the cooked pasta and add it to the skillet, tossing to combine with the steak and sauce.
Serve the pasta hot, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
